title: Abstract Wikipedia Frame Schema — Relational: Spatial Relation
description: JSON shape for the spatial relation frame, corresponding to the `relation.spatial` family in the frame inventory. This frame captures locative, topological, directional, and proximity relations between entities or locations (e.g. 'X is in Y', 'X is near Y', 'X is north of Y').

frame_type.description: Canonical frame family identifier for spatial relation frames.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this frame instance (e.g. internal row ID, page ID, or source statement cluster ID).

subject.description: Entity or location being located (X). This is the item whose spatial position is being asserted.

reference.description: Reference entity or location (Y) relative to which the subject is located.

relation.description: Coarse spatial relation label such as 'in', 'within', 'inside', 'near', 'adjacent_to', 'north_of', 'south_of', 'east_of', 'west_of', 'on', 'at', 'between', or 'across_from'.

distance.description: Optional explicit distance between subject and reference, represented as a Quantity-like object (e.g. 50 km).

region_lemma.description: Optional lemma-like label describing a salient subregion, sub-area, or directional zone relevant to the relation (e.g. 'northern', 'central', 'coastal', 'downtown').

time_span.description: Preferred field for the time span during which the relation holds, when the relation is time-sensitive or historical.

time.description: Legacy alias for `time_span`. Prefer `time_span` for new data.

certainty.description: Optional confidence value in [0,1], where 1.0 indicates maximal confidence.

source_id.description: Optional opaque identifier for the source statement, triple, row, or provenance record.

qualifiers.description: Optional language-neutral qualifiers or caveats such as 'administratively', 'historically', 'approximately', or 'by_road'.

attributes.description: Open-ended structured properties that may influence downstream planning or realization without being promoted to first-class fields.

extra.description: Opaque metadata preserved for debugging, traceability, or source-specific payloads.

EntityRef.description: Lightweight reference to a discourse entity, following the general shape used across frame schemas.

id.description: Stable identifier for the entity, e.g. Wikidata QID, local database ID, or URI.

name.description: Preferred surface name or label of the entity.

gender.description: Optional coarse gender category for human entities.

entity_type.description: Optional coarse type hint such as 'person', 'organization', 'place', 'city', 'country', 'river', or 'mountain'.

features.description: Optional structured feature dictionary used by downstream components.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata associated with the entity reference.

LocationRef.description: Lightweight location-oriented reference used when the locative nature of the argument matters explicitly.

id.description: Stable identifier for the location (e.g. QID, geocode, URI).

name.description: Human-readable location label such as 'Paris', 'Lake Victoria', or 'Western Europe'.

kind.description: Optional location kind such as 'city', 'country', 'region', 'river', 'mountain', or 'building'.

country_code.description: Optional ISO country code when applicable.

features.description: Optional structured feature dictionary useful for locative realization or downstream planning.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata associated with the location reference.

TimeSpan.description: Coarse time span or single date used to anchor historical or time-sensitive spatial relations.

start_year.description: Starting year, or the main year of interest for a point-like temporal anchor.

end_year.description: Optional end year for multi-year spans.

approximate.description: If true, the time span is approximate or inferred.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata or source-specific temporal payload.

Quantity.description: Generic numeric quantity with optional unit, qualifier, and source-facing metadata.

kind.description: Coarse semantic type of the quantity, e.g. 'measure', 'count', or 'percentage'.

as_of.description: Optional temporal anchor or observation context. May be any JSON value, including a year, string label, object, array, or null.

scope.description: Optional textual scope such as 'by_road', 'as_the_crow_flies', or 'within_city_limits'.

meta.description: Arbitrary additional metadata for the quantity.

Unit.description: Measurement unit compatible with generic quantity objects.

code.description: Canonical unit code such as 'km', 'm', 'mi', or 'ft'.

system.description: Optional system label such as 'SI', 'imperial', or 'custom'.
